reasoning_chain_text: 1. Identify California counties meeting the 2022 Area Median Income threshold, then keep the ones that also meet the 2023 threshold.
2. From the remaining counties, keep those with women, infants, and children average cost per family at or above the 2018 threshold and the 2020 threshold.
3. From the remaining counties, keep those with a 2015 perioperative hemorrhage or hematoma observed rate at or above the threshold.
4. From the remaining counties, keep those with geographical shape area of at least 3 billion square meters.
5. For the remaining county, determine its county seat.
6. Determine the bay on the southern shore of that county seat.
7. Using the bay identified in step 6, determine which ocean it connects to.
original_final_question: Identify a specific California county seat based on a set of health and housing statistics. I'm looking for a county that had an Area Median Income of at least $142,800 in 2022 and $147,900 in 2023. In terms of assistance costs, this county should have had a women, infants and children average cost per family of $570 or more in 2018 and $700 or more in 2020. It also needs to show a 2015 perioperative hemorrhage or hematoma observed rate of 19 or higher and have a geographical shape area of at least 3 billion square meters. Once you identify the county that matches all these figures, could you tell me which ocean the bay on the southern shore of its county seat connects to? Only report that ocean. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: Node 1: 2022 income limits - AMI >= 142800 -> {Santa Clara, Marin, San Francisco, San Mateo, Alameda, Contra Costa}
Node 2: 2023 income limits - AMI >= 147900 -> {Santa Clara, Marin, San Francisco, San Mateo, Alameda, Contra Costa}
Intersection -> {Alameda, Contra Costa, Marin, San Francisco, San Mateo, Santa Clara}
Node 3: 2018 WIC Avg Cost per Family >= 570 -> {Alameda, Contra Costa, San Francisco, Santa Clara}
Node 4: 2020 WIC Avg Cost per Family >= 700 -> {Alameda, San Francisco, San Mateo, Santa Clara}
Node 5: 2015 PSI 27 ObsRate >= 19 -> {San Francisco, Santa Clara}
Node 7: Santa Clara County seat -> San Jose
Node 8: San Jose on southern shore of -> San Francisco Bay
Node 9: San Francisco Bay connects to -> Pacific Ocean

ideal_code.intent: Which counties have AMI >= 142800 in the 2022 income limits data? (Filter: AMI >= 142800; list County and AMI.)
ideal_code.code:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/income-limits-by-county/files/2022-income-limits.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

df["AMI"] = pd.to_numeric(df["AMI"], errors="coerce")
result = df.loc[df["AMI"] >= 142800, ["County", "AMI"]].copy()
result = result.sort_values(["AMI", "County"], ascending=[False, True])
answer = {row["County"]: int(row["AMI"]) for _, row in result.iterrows()}

ideal_code.intent: Among the counties from hop 1, which have Average Cost per Family >= $570 in the 2018 WIC redemptions data? (Filter: Obligation Year and Month == 2018; Vendor County in <hop 1>; parse Average Cost per Family; keep >= 570.)
ideal_code.code:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/california-women-infants-and-children-program-redemption-by-county-967e9/files/2010-2018-part-a-wic-redemptions-by-vendor-county-with-family-counts.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

counties = ["Santa Clara", "Marin", "San Francisco", "San Mateo", "Alameda", "Contra Costa"]
df["Obligation Year and Month"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Obligation Year and Month"], errors="coerce")
filtered = df.loc[
    df["Obligation Year and Month"].eq(2018) & df["Vendor County"].isin(counties),
    ["Vendor County", "Average Cost per Family"],
].copy()
filtered["Average Cost per Family"] = pd.to_numeric(
    filtered["Average Cost per Family"].astype(str).str.replace(r"[^0-9.\-]", "", regex=True),
    errors="coerce",
)
answer = (
    filtered.loc[filtered["Average Cost per Family"] >= 570, "Vendor County"]
    .dropna()
    .drop_duplicates()
    .sort_values()
    .tolist()
)

ideal_code.intent: Among the counties from hop 1, which have Average Cost per Family >= $700 in the 2020 WIC redemptions data? (Filter: Obligation Year and Month == 2020; Vendor County in <hop 1>; parse Average Cost per Family; keep >= 700.)

ideal_code.intent: Among the counties from hop 2, which have ObsRate >= 19 for PSI 27 (Perioperative Hemorrhage or Hematoma) in 2015? (Filter: Year == 2015; PSI == 27; County in <hop 2>; read ObsRate; keep >= 19.)
ideal_code.code:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/hospitalization-counts-and-rates-of-selected-adverse-hospital-events-by-california-county-67a4a/files/ca-oshpd-adveventhospitalizationspsi-county2005-2015q3.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

counties = ["Alameda", "San Francisco", "Santa Clara"]
df["Year"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Year"], errors="coerce")
df["PSI"] = pd.to_numeric(df["PSI"], errors="coerce")
df["ObsRate"] = pd.to_numeric(df["ObsRate"], errors="coerce")
filtered = df.loc[
    df["Year"].eq(2015) & df["PSI"].eq(27) & df["County"].isin(counties),
    ["County", "ObsRate"],
].copy()
answer = (
    filtered.loc[filtered["ObsRate"] >= 19, "County"]
    .dropna()
    .drop_duplicates()
    .sort_values()
    .tolist()
)

ideal_code.intent: Among the counties from hop 2, which have Shape__Area >= 3000000000 in the California county boundaries data? (Filter: COUNTY_NAME in <hop 2>; read Shape__Area; keep >= 3000000000.)
ideal_code.code:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/california-county-boundaries-bd0f6/files/California_County_Boundaries_9101614255844818638.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

counties = ["Alameda", "San Francisco", "Santa Clara"]
df["Shape__Area"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Shape__Area"], errors="coerce")
filtered = df.loc[
    df["COUNTY_NAME"].isin(counties),
    ["COUNTY_NAME", "Shape__Area"],
].copy()
answer = (
    filtered.loc[filtered["Shape__Area"] >= 3000000000, "COUNTY_NAME"]
    .dropna()
    .drop_duplicates()
    .sort_values()
    .tolist()
)

ideal_query.intent: Which counties have AMI >= 142800 in the 2022 income limits data? (Filter: AMI >= 142800; list County and AMI.)
ideal_query.sql:
WITH filtered AS (
  SELECT "County" AS county, TRY_CAST("AMI" AS BIGINT) AS ami
  FROM t
  WHERE TRY_CAST("AMI" AS BIGINT) >= 142800
  ORDER BY ami DESC, county ASC
)
SELECT json_group_object(county, ami) AS answer FROM filtered

ideal_query.sql:
WITH filtered AS (
  SELECT "County" AS county, TRY_CAST("AMI" AS BIGINT) AS ami
  FROM t
  WHERE TRY_CAST("AMI" AS BIGINT) >= 147900
  ORDER BY ami DESC, county ASC
)
SELECT json_group_object(county, ami) AS answer FROM filtered

ideal_query.intent: Among the counties from hop 1, which have Average Cost per Family >= $570 in the 2018 WIC redemptions data? (Filter: Obligation Year and Month == 2018; Vendor County in <hop 1>; parse Average Cost per Family; keep >= 570.)
ideal_query.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT "Vendor County" AS answer
FROM t
WHERE TRY_CAST("Obligation Year and Month" AS INTEGER) = 2018
  AND "Vendor County" IN ('Santa Clara', 'Marin', 'San Francisco', 'San Mateo', 'Alameda', 'Contra Costa')
  AND TRY_CAST(regexp_replace(CAST("Average Cost per Family" AS VARCHAR), '[^0-9.\-]', '', 'g') AS DOUBLE) >= 570
ORDER BY "Vendor County"

ideal_query.intent: Among the counties from hop 1, which have Average Cost per Family >= $700 in the 2020 WIC redemptions data? (Filter: Obligation Year and Month == 2020; Vendor County in <hop 1>; parse Average Cost per Family; keep >= 700.)
ideal_query.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT "Vendor County" AS answer
FROM t
WHERE TRY_CAST("Obligation Year and Month" AS INTEGER) = 2020
  AND "Vendor County" IN ('Santa Clara', 'Marin', 'San Francisco', 'San Mateo', 'Alameda', 'Contra Costa')
  AND TRY_CAST(regexp_replace(CAST("Average Cost per Family" AS VARCHAR), '[^0-9.\-]', '', 'g') AS DOUBLE) >= 700
ORDER BY "Vendor County"

ideal_query.intent: Among the counties from hop 2, which have ObsRate >= 19 for PSI 27 (Perioperative Hemorrhage or Hematoma) in 2015? (Filter: Year == 2015; PSI == 27; County in <hop 2>; read ObsRate; keep >= 19.)
ideal_query.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT "County" AS answer
FROM t
WHERE TRY_CAST("Year" AS INTEGER) = 2015
  AND TRY_CAST("PSI" AS INTEGER) = 27
  AND "County" IN ('Alameda', 'San Francisco', 'Santa Clara')
  AND TRY_CAST("ObsRate" AS DOUBLE) >= 19
ORDER BY "County"

ideal_query.intent: Among the counties from hop 2, which have Shape__Area >= 3000000000 in the California county boundaries data? (Filter: COUNTY_NAME in <hop 2>; read Shape__Area; keep >= 3000000000.)
ideal_query.sql:
SELECT DISTINCT "COUNTY_NAME" AS answer
FROM t
WHERE "COUNTY_NAME" IN ('Alameda', 'San Francisco', 'Santa Clara')
  AND TRY_CAST("Shape__Area" AS DOUBLE) >= 3000000000
ORDER BY "COUNTY_NAME"
